name: Dallas Wheelchair Transport
recent_news: Operates as 'Discount Wheelchair Transportation Service' providing non-emergency medical transport (NEMT) for disabled persons in Dallas/Plano/Frisco area; no evidence of school bus or student transport contracts.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus operator; pure NEMT/wheelchair van service with 2 employees. Not a fit for Summit tuck-in strategy.

name: Chariot Of Care LLC
recent_news: Provider listed as non-emergency medical transport (van) in Plymouth, NC; listed website (Procraftflorida.com) appears mismatched/unrelated. Facebook page exists but minimal activity. Not identified as a school-bus contractor.
fit_notes: Micro NEMT van operator with 2 employees in rural NC; not a school-bus contractor and not a fit for Summit tuck-in.

name: Mary's School Bus
recent_news: Florida corporation 'Mary's School Bus, Corp' DISSOLVED on 03/11/2024 per Sunbiz. FMCSA carrier under owner-operator Claudia M Irias (USDOT 2068295) registered since Aug 2010. Hialeah address 424 W 13th St.
fit_notes: Corp dissolved March 2024; only an owner-operator level FMCSA registration remains. Too small/likely defunct for Summit tuck-in.

name: Romy School Bus Transportation
recent_news: No web/news presence found; not identified on Miami-Dade DOT approved contractor lists in surfaced results. Likely a micro owner-operator subcontractor in Homestead.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~4 employees) with no web footprint; not a viable Summit tuck-in target.

name: Cicada Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely Sussex/Kent County DE district subcontract (not on Milford SD's 9-contractor list)
recent_news: Operates at 228 Cicada Lane, Milford DE 19963 (302-422-9510), categorized School Bus Transportation Services on Google Maps. NOT named on Milford School District's current contractor bus list (which has 9 contractors). Likely serves Capital, Lake Forest, or Cape Henlopen districts as a small contractor.
fit_notes: Independent family-owned DE school-bus contractor; very small (~6 employees, est. 2-3 buses). Below Summit's typical tuck-in scale but Delaware fits Summit footprint; could be a roll-up add-on if multiple DE micro-contractors are acquired together.
